rent.note: furnished 1BR or private room in Panajachel; range $300–$800

utilities.note: electric + water + internet (25–80 Mbps at coworking); lake climate, no A/C needed

groceries.note: home cooking; local market produce very cheap

transit.note: lanchas between villages + tuk-tuks in Panajachel

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly $50–$150; Sababa Resort coworking notable option

simData.note: Claro/Tigo 30-day plan; coverage decent in Panajachel

misc.note: gym if available, toiletries, entertainment, kayaking, excursions, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base.

evidence.quote: Most digital nomads find they can live comfortably on $800 to $1,200 USD per month

evidence.claim: accommodation $300–$800/month for private room or apartment
evidence.quote: Accommodation ranges from $300-$800/month for a private room or apartment
Coworking costs $50-$150/month for a dedicated desk. Internet speeds are 25-80 Mbps at coworking spaces and many cafes

evidence.claim: Panajachel most connected town on the lake but pricier than other villages
evidence.quote: Panajachel is the largest and most connected town, with ATMs, supermarkets, and the best internet infrastructure on the lake. However, it tends to be more expensive than other lake villages
